source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Explore and compare the differences between things that are living, dead, and things that have never been alive; identify habitats and simple food chains.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack combines classification evidence, habitat suitability, microhabitat observation and simple food-chain reasoning without presenting habitats as decorative matching games.

objective.child_goal: I can use evidence to classify things and explain how a habitat meets needs.
objective.statement: Classify living, dead and never-alive things, relate organisms to suitable habitats and construct simple food chains.
misconceptions: Thinks movement is the only sign that something is living.
Classifies all non-moving or brown things as dead.
Matches an organism to a habitat only because colours or pictures look similar.
Draws food-chain arrows as showing which animal is bigger or which way it moves.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Alive now and carrying out life processes.
vocabulary.audio_script: A living thing is alive now and needs things such as food, water or light.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Evidence icons for growth, nutrition and response.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Was once living but is no longer alive.
vocabulary.audio_script: A dead thing was alive in the past.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A fallen leaf linked back to a living tree.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Has never been a living thing.
vocabulary.audio_script: A rock has never been alive.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A rock card with no life-history link.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A place where a plant or animal lives and gets what it needs.
vocabulary.audio_script: A habitat provides the conditions a living thing needs.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Shelter, food, water and light icons around a place.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A sequence showing what is eaten and where food energy passes.
vocabulary.audio_script: A food chain shows food energy passing from one living thing to another.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Plant, caterpillar and bird cards joined by labelled arrows.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Look closely at the mystery item. What can you observe, and what can you not yet know?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Name visible evidence first. A still picture may not show every sign of life.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A seed, toy insect and leaf shown one at a time with evidence icons hidden.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort living, dead and never alive. Give one reason for each choice.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish life history, not movement or appearance, as the classification basis.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ask: is it alive now, was it once alive, or has it never been alive?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three labelled trays with a life-history timeline.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A dormant seed may look still. A wooden spoon was made from something once living.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Address difficult boundary cases using age-appropriate evidence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Still does not always mean dead. Wood came from a living tree, but the spoon is not alive.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A seed germination sequence and tree-to-wood-to-spoon sequence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Why might a woodlouse live under a damp log?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check habitat suitability through conditions and needs rather than visual matching.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The log gives shelter and the damp place helps the woodlouse avoid drying out.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A log microhabitat with moisture, shade, shelter and food evidence toggles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose a suitable habitat, then build a food chain found there.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect organisms, habitat resources and feeding relationships.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Check what each organism needs and what each consumer eats.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Habitat cards feed a three-position chain builder.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip what the arrow means in grass to snail to thrush.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Confirm food-chain order and energy-transfer meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The arrow points from the food to the living thing that eats it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Each arrow gains a 'is eaten by' label when selected.

manipulatives.purpose: Classify using current life, past life and never-alive evidence, including carefully chosen boundary examples.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children inspect an item, open optional evidence cards, then tap, drag, keyboard-step or switch-scan it into a tray and select a reason.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large cards, alt descriptions, tactile-style texture symbols, no timer, evidence read-aloud, and an 'not enough evidence yet' option prevent forced guessing.

manipulatives.purpose: Match organisms to habitat conditions and link producer-consumer relationships.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children toggle need/condition icons, place organism cards, then build a three-link chain with arrows whose meaning can be replayed.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Icons always include text, colour is redundant, all actions have single-tap and keyboard alternatives, and reduced-motion mode uses static highlighted links.

practice.interleaving_notes: Begin with clear life-status examples, then add boundary cases. Teach habitat needs before interleaving local microhabitats and simple producer-consumer food chains.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Classify clear living, dead and never-alive examples using stated evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: familiar item + observation + one life-history evidence card + reason choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid ambiguous images and accept 'need more evidence' where a still image is insufficient.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Reason about seeds, wood, fallen leaves and manufactured natural materials.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: item + before/after life-history sequence + classify current status
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review must check scientific precision and age-appropriate wording.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Select a habitat because it provides relevant food, water, shelter, light or conditions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: organism + two or three habitats + needs/conditions evidence + because explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use plausible distractors and local UK examples without implying every individual lives identically.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply observations from small local habitats to explain organism distribution.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: microhabitat comparison + moisture/light/shelter observations + likely organism choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Frame as evidence-based likelihood, not a guarantee; include safe fieldwork prompts.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build and explain simple chains beginning with a producer.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: habitat-linked producer + primary consumer + predator + labelled arrows
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Arrow semantics must remain consistent: food to eater / energy transfer.

body.prompt: A dry seed is not moving. Which evidence best helps decide whether it is living?
choices: It can germinate and grow when conditions are right
It is brown
It fits in a tray
It is not moving now
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: It can germinate and grow when conditions are right

hints: Movement is not the only sign of life.
Think about what the seed can do in suitable conditions.
question_variants.explanation: A dormant seed is living because it can germinate and grow when it receives suitable conditions.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "It can germinate and grow when conditions are right". A dormant seed is living because it can germinate and grow when it receives suitable conditions.
feedback.repair: Movement is not the only sign of life. Think about what the seed can do in suitable conditions. Use that clue to repair movement only life, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this life status sort, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "It can germinate and grow when conditions are right": A dormant seed is living because it can germinate and grow when it receives suitable conditions.
feedback.misconception_check: movement only life
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A wooden spoon is not alive. Which group best describes its material's history?
choices: Made from something once living
Living now
Never connected to anything living
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: Made from something once living

hints: Wood comes from trees.
The spoon itself does not grow or need food.
question_variants.explanation: The spoon is not living, but its wood came from a tree that was once living.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "Made from something once living". The spoon is not living, but its wood came from a tree that was once living.
feedback.repair: Wood comes from trees. The spoon itself does not grow or need food. Use that clue to repair still or brown means dead, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this life status sort, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "Made from something once living": The spoon is not living, but its wood came from a tree that was once living.
feedback.misconception_check: still or brown means dead
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Why is the space under a damp log a suitable microhabitat for a woodlouse?
choices: It provides damp conditions and shelter
Its colours match the woodlouse
It is the biggest place
Everything living must live under logs
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: It provides damp conditions and shelter

hints: Think about conditions, not matching colours.
The log can reduce light and help hold moisture.
question_variants.explanation: The damp, sheltered conditions help meet a woodlouse's needs.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "It provides damp conditions and shelter". The damp, sheltered conditions help meet a woodlouse's needs.
feedback.repair: Think about conditions, not matching colours. The log can reduce light and help hold moisture. Use that clue to repair picture matching habitat, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this habitat evidence map, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "It provides damp conditions and shelter": The damp, sheltered conditions help meet a woodlouse's needs.
feedback.misconception_check: picture matching habitat
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Build the food chain and choose what each arrow means.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Start with the plant.
Point from the food to the eater.
question_variants.explanation: Grass is eaten by the snail, and the snail is eaten by the thrush, so the arrows point from food to eater.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is sequence: "grass", "snail", "thrush"; arrow label: "is eaten by". Grass is eaten by the snail, and the snail is eaten by the thrush, so the arrows point from food to eater.
feedback.repair: Start with the plant. Point from the food to the eater. Use that clue to repair arrow direction confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this food chain build, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports sequence: "grass", "snail", "thrush"; arrow label: "is eaten by": Grass is eaten by the snail, and the snail is eaten by the thrush, so the arrows point from food to eater.
feedback.misconception_check: arrow direction confusion
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Life-status mission 1: classify potted bean plant using the evidence, not appearance.
body.observation: It grows and needs water and light.
choices: living now
was once living
was once living material
never alive
body.narration_script: Classify potted bean plant using the evidence card.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move the child's named card or record an indicated explanation without supplying the science decision.
body.picture_sort_route: Large clear item cards with concise alt descriptions and evidence revealed separately from decorative appearance.
body.habitat_map_route: Plain need-condition table or labelled habitat picture with one evidence link at a time.
body.chain_builder_route: Three large slots with persistent producer and “is eaten by” arrow labels.
body.observation_route: Use safe look-only local observation, dated picture records or provided evidence; no organism handling is required.
body.sensory_safe_route: No sudden sounds, flashing, graphic death/predation, compulsory textures or startling animal movement; quiet static mode is complete.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter cards, plain backgrounds, generous spacing and evidence icons paired with text.
body.processing_route: Follow observe–evidence–decide–explain, revealing only one comparison or relationship at a time and preserving correct work.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed choices can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: help a calm habitat explorer organise one evidence link
gamification.reward: one explorer leaf for a careful sort, map, observation or chain
gamification.retry_message: Your correct evidence stays. Choose another clue or route and continue.

expected_answer.value: living now

hints: Ask whether it is alive now or has a past connection to life.
It grows and needs water and light.
question_variants.explanation: potted bean plant is classified as living now. It grows and needs water and light.

feedback.correct: Evidence supports “living now”.
feedback.repair: Keep the evidence card open, remove colour and movement clues, and compare only the current-life and life-history questions.
feedback.science_evidence: potted bean plant is classified as living now. It grows and needs water and light.
feedback.support_message: Picture sorting, mapping, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations carry equal evidence; speed, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the appearance only classification idea against the task evidence.

body.picture_sort_route: Large clear item cards with concise alt descriptions and evidence revealed separately from decorative appearance.
body.habitat_map_route: Plain need-condition table or labelled habitat picture with one evidence link at a time.
body.chain_builder_route: Three large slots with persistent producer and “is eaten by” arrow labels.
body.observation_route: Use safe look-only local observation, dated picture records or provided evidence; no organism handling is required.
body.sensory_safe_route: No sudden sounds, flashing, graphic death/predation, compulsory textures or startling animal movement; quiet static mode is complete.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter cards, plain backgrounds, generous spacing and evidence icons paired with text.
body.processing_route: Follow observe–evidence–decide–explain, revealing only one comparison or relationship at a time and preserving correct work.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed choices can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

feedback.misconception_check: Check the current appearance erases history idea against the task evidence.

feedback.support_message: Picture sorting, mapping, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations carry equal evidence; speed, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the arrow direction confusion idea against the task evidence.

body.prompt: Producer mission 43: which card starts the hedgerow food chain by making its own food using light?
choices: seeds
mouse
owl
body.producer_definition: green plant or alga makes its own food using light
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move the child's named card or record an indicated explanation without supplying the science decision.
body.picture_sort_route: Large clear item cards with concise alt descriptions and evidence revealed separately from decorative appearance.
body.habitat_map_route: Plain need-condition table or labelled habitat picture with one evidence link at a time.
body.chain_builder_route: Three large slots with persistent producer and “is eaten by” arrow labels.
body.observation_route: Use safe look-only local observation, dated picture records or provided evidence; no organism handling is required.
body.sensory_safe_route: No sudden sounds, flashing, graphic death/predation, compulsory textures or startling animal movement; quiet static mode is complete.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter cards, plain backgrounds, generous spacing and evidence icons paired with text.
body.processing_route: Follow observe–evidence–decide–explain, revealing only one comparison or relationship at a time and preserving correct work.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed choices can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: help a calm habitat explorer organise one evidence link
gamification.reward: one explorer leaf for a careful sort, map, observation or chain
gamification.retry_message: Your correct evidence stays. Choose another clue or route and continue.

expected_answer.value: seeds

hints: Start with the green plant or alga producer.
Read each arrow as “is eaten by”.
question_variants.explanation: seeds is the producer. mouse and owl are consumers because they eat plants or other animals.

feedback.correct: Food-chain evidence is consistent. seeds is the producer. mouse and owl are consumers because they eat plants or other animals.
feedback.repair: Keep the producer first, place one eater at a time and speak or reveal “is eaten by” over every arrow.
feedback.science_evidence: seeds is the producer. mouse and owl are consumers because they eat plants or other animals.
feedback.support_message: Picture sorting, mapping, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations carry equal evidence; speed, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the largest animal starts chain idea against the task evidence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks movement is the only sign that something is living.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child classifies a dormant seed or plant as non-living because it is still.
repair_sequence: Compare a moving toy with a germinating seed.
Classify using growth and need evidence, not movement alone.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child correctly explains why a new non-moving organism can be living.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Classifies all non-moving or brown things as dead.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child puts a rock, wooden object and dormant seed in the same group.
repair_sequence: Trace whether each item was ever alive.
Use living-now, once-living and never-alive timelines.
Re-sort with one reason per item.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child distinguishes dead/once-living material from never-alive matter and a dormant living seed.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Matches an organism to a habitat only because colours or pictures look similar.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child cites appearance instead of food, water, light, shelter or physical conditions.
repair_sequence: Reveal organism needs and measured conditions.
Choose again and complete a because statement.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child selects a new habitat using a relevant need-condition link.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Draws food-chain arrows as showing which animal is bigger or which way it moves.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child reverses arrows or explains them as travel direction.
repair_sequence: Say 'is eaten by' while drawing each arrow.
Trace energy from producer through consumers.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child builds and reads a new three-link chain with arrows from food to eater.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use still habitat scenes, optional ambient sound and no sudden predator animations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace germination, habitat and feeding animations with labelled before/after panels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate item descriptions, evidence cards and arrow meanings with replay while keeping text visible.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short evidence statements, consistent icons and one classification or relationship per screen.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Follow observe-evidence-decide-explain and needs-conditions-match routines with unused controls hidden.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow evidence cards to remain open, unlimited thinking time, undo and side-by-side comparisons.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Offer 'not enough evidence yet', preserve correct reasoning and avoid graphic death or predation imagery.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach living, dead, never alive, habitat, shelter, damp, producer and eaten by with pictures and gestures.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires evidence-based life-status classification and habitat matching; secure requires boundary-case reasoning and retained construction/explanation of simple habitat-linked food chains.
evidence.parent_summary: Can classify living, dead and never-alive things, explain why a habitat suits an organism and build a simple food chain.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Uses observations and life history, links needs to habitat conditions and reads arrows consistently from food to eater.
next_actions: Use safe local microhabitat observations to collect real evidence.
Revisit ambiguous classifications with life-history timelines.
Extend secure chains by comparing organisms within the same habitat.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 living-things-and-habitats pack with a deterministic 220-variant pilot bank. Four curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks cover evidence-based living/once-living/never-alive classification, age-appropriate boundary cases, habitats and microhabitats, how conditions meet basic needs, local observation and seasonal variation, plant-animal dependence, simple producer-consumer food chains, misconception repair and transfer. Food-chain arrows consistently mean food is eaten by eater. Every generated task includes picture sorting, habitat/evidence mapping, chain building or safe observation routes, sensory-safe reduced-load SEND supports, alternative inputs, rich feedback and pressure-free exploration without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected narration references ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent science, fieldwork safety, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
